'''Drackmar'''<ref name="TEA" /> was the [[Drackmarian]] homeworld, located in the [[Drackmar system/Legends|Drackmar system]],<ref name="TCSWE" /> in the [[Quelii sector/Legends|Quelii sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of Drackmar was composed of [[Methane/Legends|methane]],<ref name="TCSWE" /> requiring native Drackmarians to wear special methane helmets when traveling off-world.<ref name="TCoPL">''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]''</ref> On the other hand, oxygen-breathing species could use environmentally-sealed vehicles, such as the [[Orbitblade-2000]], to visit the planet.<ref name="TBHC">''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]''</ref>

==History==
Drackmar was the homeworld of the Drackmarian [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] who conquered several neighboring worlds<ref name="TEA" /><ref name="TTaBS">''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]''</ref> in the Quelii sector,<ref name="EAOC" /> including [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]]<ref name="TEA" /> and [[Morseer]].<ref name="TTaBS" />

During the [[Mandalorian Wars]], Drackmar and the Drackmarians joined the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

The Drackmarians were eventually defeated by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="TTaBS" /> and the Drackmar system was integrated in the [[Quelii Oversector]]. The system was later part of Warlord [[Zsinj]]'s [[Zsinj's Empire|empire]] and, after the death of Zsinj, was [[Post–Zsinj campaigns|annexed]] by loyal Imperial forces. The Drackmar system was conquered by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] in the early stages of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Although the Drackmarians and the Drackmar system were created in the [[1994]] novel ''[[The Courtship of Princess Leia]]'',<ref name="TCoPL" /> and were later mentioned in several other books,<ref name="TCSWE" /> the world of Drackmar was only mentioned in the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''.<ref name="TEA" />
